title: BlackBerry's Quiet Comeback: The Software Powering 275 Million Cars Turns Heads
text: BlackBerry is experiencing a quiet comeback driven by its QNX automotive software technology that powers 275 million vehicles worldwide. The company's stock surged following reports of renewed momentum in its automotive business. QNX achieved record Q4 revenue of $268 million, growing 20% year-over-year with a $950 million royalty backlog. Recent automotive wins include Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Volvo, Leapmotor, and TKMS. BlackBerry is expanding QNX beyond traditional automotive into emerging markets like robotics, industrial automation, and medical instrumentation. The company is developing the Alloy Core platform to move deeper into automotive software stacks. Management is in active discussions with major OEMs like Mercedes-Benz, with potential ASP multiples far exceeding the core OS pricing. The turnaround story is being powered by strong fundamentals and positioning in high-growth markets, with CEO John Giamatteo stating the company is no longer in transition but positioned as a growth story.
